commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human review should decide the sandbox default expansion and require live channel proof; there is no narrow automated repair to apply to the current patch.; Needs attention: The diff broadens the default sandbox tool allowlist to include a channel-sending/managing tool, so it needs maintainer acceptance as a sandbox policy expansion. (sha=24c41971676474f4ad8e7c5364c6920caebc5e47)
commands.review_summary:
Adds `message` to `DEFAULT_TOOL_ALLOW` and adds a sandbox tool-policy regression test asserting default sandbox configs allow the message tool.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +22. Total +23 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main's default sandbox allowlist excludes `message`, and the sandbox policy resolver uses that default when no override exists. I did not run a live Telegram or Discord repro, so the end-to-end no-delivery symptom remains source-backed rather than live-reproduced here.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review should decide the sandbox default expansion and require live channel proof; there is no narrow automated repair to apply to the current patch.

target.title: fix(sandbox): include message tool in default sandbox allowlist
target.body:
Fixes #88253

## Summary

A sandboxed channel-bound agent had no way to deliver replies to the originating channel. `message` was absent from `DEFAULT_TOOL_ALLOW`, so the only available messaging tool was `sessions_send` — which routes internally via the webchat lane and is never delivered to a real channel.

Adding `message` to the default allowlist restores expected behaviour: a sandboxed agent can use the `message` tool to reply to Telegram/Discord/etc. without requiring an explicit `tools.sandbox.tools.alsoAllow: [\"message\"]` override in every agent config. Explicit `deny` entries still take precedence per the existing policy.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** `DEFAULT_TOOL_ALLOW` lacked `\"message\"`, so the gateway log showed `tool policy removed … message …` for sandboxed agents. Sandboxed Telegram agents replied to the internal webchat lane instead of the inbound channel, producing silent no-delivery.

**Real environment tested:** Linux x86_64, Node.js v22.22.0, HEAD `24c41971676474f4ad8e7c5364c6920caebc5e47` based on `origin/main` `462e315953371f7fce096a71d0f599c72036f496`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \  --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts \  src/agents/sandbox/tool-policy.test.ts \  --reporter=verbose --testTimeout=10000
```

**Evidence after fix:**

```
 ✓ sandbox/tool-policy > includes message tool in the default sandbox allowlist
 ✓ sandbox/tool-policy > merges sandbox alsoAllow into the default sandbox allowlist
 ✓ sandbox/tool-policy > keeps explicit sandbox deny precedence over allow and alsoAllow
 … (8 more)
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  11 passed (11)
   Duration  1.40s
```

New regression test confirms that `message` is in the resolved allow list and `isToolAllowed` returns `true` for a default sandbox config. Existing deny-precedence test is unchanged (explicit `deny: [\"message\"]` still blocks it).

**Observed result after fix:** `DEFAULT_TOOL_ALLOW` includes `\"message\"`. A sandboxed agent's resolved allow list contains `\"message\"` without any `alsoAllow` override.

**What was not tested:** End-to-end sandboxed Telegram → message tool → real channel delivery in a live gateway. The policy-resolution logic is exercised by unit tests against real config structs.
target.author: alix-007
